title: dalbergia stevensonii
intro: Dalbergia stevensonii, also known as Stevens' rosewood, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia stevensonii lie in its heartwood, which contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia stevensonii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical applications for various health benefits.
